Djamé Seddah

Research in NLP, Robust Language Modeling, LLM and Model Safety, Low Ressource Languages and Specialized domains

Home Research People Publications Models and Datasets Teaching Contact

Publications

This page preserves the long publication list extracted from the archived site. It remains intentionally close to the source material, with only light cleanup for GitHub Pages.

2026

  • Triggers Hijack Language Circuits: A Mechanistic Analysis of Backdoor Behaviors in Large Language Models Tho Lasnier, Wissam Antoun, Francis Kulumba, Djamé Seddah. 2026. arXiv preprint
  • When Tables Go Crazy: Evaluating Multimodal Models on French Financial Documents Virginie Mouilleron, Tho Lasnier, Djamé Seddah. 2026. arXiv preprint
  • Disentangling meaning from language in LLM-based machine translation. Tho Lasnier, Armel Zebaze, Djamé Seddah, Rachel Bawden, Benot Sagot. 2026. arxiv preprint
  • Leveraging Wikidata for Geographically Informed Sociocultural Bias Dataset Creation: Application to Latin America Yannis Karmim, Renato Pino, Hernan Contreras, Hernan Lira, Sebastian Cifuentes, Simon Escoffier, Luis Mart, Djamé Seddah, Valentin Barrire. 2026. To appear in proceedings of the Workshop on Multilingual and Multicultural Evaluation, colocated with EACL 2026 (MME@EACL). paper
  • Multilingual, Multimodal Pipeline for Creating Authentic and Structured Fact-Checked Claim DatasetZ. Melce Hsnbeyi, Virginie Mouilleron, Leonie Uhling, Daniel Foppe, Tatjana Scheffler, Djamé Seddah. 2026. To appear in the proceedings of the Fifteenth biennial Language Resources and Evaluation Conference (LREC 2026). pdf

2025

  • ModernBERT or DeBERTaV3? Examining Architecture and Data Influence on Transformer Encoder Models Performance. Wissam Antoun, Benot Sagot, Djamé Seddah. 2025. In proceedings of AACL-IJCNLP paper
  • Integrating Semantic Representations in a Cross-Modal Approach to Fact-Checking Melce Husunbeyi, Djamé Seddah, Tatjana Scheffler. Proceedings of the 4th ACM International Workshop on Multimedia AI against Disinformation (MAD25), colocated with the ACM International Conference on Multimedia Retrieval (ICMR25). paper
  • Beyond Dataset Creation: Critical View of Annotation Variation and Bias Probing of a Dataset for Online Radical Content Detection. Arij Riabi, Virginie Mouilleron, Menel Mahamdi, Wissam Antoun, and Djamé Seddah. 2025. In Proceedings of the 31st International Conference on Computational Linguistics (COLING), pages 86408663, Abu Dhabi, UAE. pdf
  • Common Ground, Diverse Roots: The Difficulty of Classifying Common Examples in Spanish Varieties Javier Alejandro Lopetegui Gonzalez, Arij Riabi, Djamé Seddah. VarDial 2025 - Twelfth Workshop on NLP for Similar Languages, Varieties and Dialects co-located with COLING 2025, Jan 2025, Abu Dhabi, United Arab Emirates hal

2024

  • Beyond Dataset Creation: Critical View of Annotation Variation and Bias Probing of a Dataset for Online Radical Content Detection. A Riabi, V Mouilleron, M Mahamdi, W Antoun, D Seddah 2024.arXiv preprint
  • Common Ground, Diverse Roots: The Difficulty of Classifying Common Examples in Spanish Varieties JA Lopetegui, A Riabi, D.Seddah arXiv preprint
  • CamemBERT 2.0: A Smarter French Language Model Aged to Perfection W Antoun, F Kulumba, R Touchent, de la Clergerie, B Sagot, D Seddah, 2024. arXiv preprint
  • Cloaked classifiers: Pseudonymization strategies on sensitive classification tasks. 2024. A Riabi, M Mahamdi, V Mouilleron, D Seddah arXiv preprint

2023

  • Data-Efficient French Language Modeling with CamemBERTa. 2023. Wissam Antoun, Benot Sagot and Djamé Seddah. To appear in Proceedings of Findings of the ACL 2023. preprint
  • Towards a Robust Detection of Language Model Generated Text: Is ChatGPT that Easy to Detect?. 2023. Wissam Antoun, Virginie Mouilleron, Benot Sagot and Djamé Seddah. To appear in Proceedings of TALN 2023. preprint
  • Enriching the NArabizi Treebank: A Multifaceted Approach to Supporting an Under-Resourced Language. Arij Riabi, Menel Mahamdi, Djamé Seddah.2023. Proceedings of the 17th Linguistic Annotation Workshop (LAW-XVII) colocated with ACL. paper
  • Multi-way Variational NMT for UGC: Improving Robustness in Zero-shot Scenarios via Mixture Density Networks. 2023. Jos Carlos Rosales Nez, Djamé Seddah, Guillaume Wisniewski. In Proceedings of The 24th Nordic Conference on Computational Linguistics (NODALIDA 2023) ( preprint (to appear))
  • Analyzing Zero-Shot transfer Scenarios across Spanish variants for Hate Speech Detection. 2023. Galo Castillo-Lpez, Arij Riabi and Djamé Seddah. In Proceedings of the Tenth Workshop on NLP for Similar Languages, Varieties and Dialects (VARDIAL), colocated with EACL 2O23. preprint (to appear)

2022

  • On the Granularity of Explanations in Model Agnostic NLP Interpretability. 2022. Yves Rychener, Xavier Renard, Djamé Seddah, Pascal Frossard, Marcin Detyniecki. In Proceedings of ECML PKDD 2022 International Workshop on eXplainable Knowledge Discovery in Data Mining (XKDD 2022), Grenoble, France. paper
  • Multilingual Auxiliary Tasks Training: Bridging the Gap between Languages for Zero-Shot Transfer of Hate Speech Detection Models. 2022. Syrielle Montariol, Arij Riabi, Djamé Seddah. Findings of AACL-IJCNLP 2022 - 2nd Conference of the Asia-Pacific Chapter of the Association for Computational Linguistics and the 12th International Joint Conference on Natural Language Processing, Nov 2022, Online, France. paper
  • The MRL 2022 Shared Task on Multilingual Clause-level Morphology. 2022. Omer Goldman, Francesco Tinner, Hila Gonen, Benjamin Muller, Victoria Basmov, Shadrack Kirimi, Lydia Nishimwe, Benot Sagot, Djamé Seddah, Reut Tsarfaty, Duygu Ataman 1st Shared Task on Multilingual Clause-level Morphology (colocated with EMNLP 2022), Dec 2022, Abu Dhabi, United Arab Emirates (to appear)
  • Treebanking user-generated content: a UD based overview of guidelines, corpora and unified recommendations . 2022. Manuela Sanguinetti, Cristina Bosco, Lauren Cassidy, zlem etinoğlu, Alessandra Teresa Cignarella, Teresa Lynn, Ines Rehbein, Josef Ruppenhofer, Djamé Seddah and Amir Zeldes . Language Resources & Evaluation (2022). DOI
  • Exploiting Inductive Bias in Transformers for Unsupervised Disentanglement of Syntax and Semantics with VAEs. 2022. Ghazi Felhi, Joseph Le Roux, Djamé Seddah. n Proceedings of the 2022 Conference of the North American Chapter of the Association for Computational Linguistics: Human Language Technologies (NAACL 2022), pages 57635776, Seattle, United States. Association for Computational Linguistics. paper
  • Fine-tuning and Sampling Strategies for Multimodal Role Labeling of Entities under Class Imbalance. 2022. Syrielle Montariol, tienne Simon, Arij Riabi and Djamé Seddah. In Proceedings of the Second Workshop on Combating Online Hostile Posts in Regional Languages during Emergency Situation (CONSTRAINTS Shared Task on Hateful meme multimodal detection) [[https://hal.inria.fr/hal-03840060 paper]
  • Tches Auxiliaires Multilingues pour le Transfert de Modles de Dtection de Discours Haineux. 2022. Arij Riabi, Syrielle Montariol, Djamé Seddah. Traitement Automatique des Langues Naturelles, Jun 2022, Avignon, France. pp.413-423. paper
  • Towards Unsupervised Content Disentanglement in Sentence Representations via Syntactic Roles. 2022. Ghazi Felhi, Joseph Le Roux, Djamé Seddah. . CtrlGen: Controllable Generative Modeling in Language and Vision, Jan 2022, virtual, France. paper?
  • PAGnol: An Extra-Large French Generative Model. 2022. Julien Launay, E. L. Tommasone, Baptiste Pannier, Franois Boniface, Amlie Chatelain, Alessandro Cappelli, Iacopo Poli, Djamé Seddah. 2022. LREC 2022. Marseille. (To appear) Arxiv preprint

2021

  • PAGnol: An Extra-Large French Generative Model. Julien Launay, E. L. Tommasone, Baptiste Pannier, Franois Boniface, Amlie Chatelain, Alessandro Cappelli, Iacopo Poli, Djamé Seddah Arxiv preprint
  • Challenging the Semi-Supervised VAE Framework for Text Classification,Ghazi Felhi, Joseph Le Roux, Djamé Seddah. In Proceedings of the Second Workshop on Insights from Negative Results in NLP, Colocated with EMNLP 2021. pdf
  • Towards Unsupervised Content Disentanglement in Sentence Representations via Syntactic Roles Ghazi Felhi, Joseph Le Roux, Djamé Seddah. In proceedings of Controllable Generative Modeling in Language and Vision, colocated with Neurips 2021. pdf

  • Noisy UGC Translation at the Character Level: Revisiting Open-Vocabulary Capabilities and Robustness of Char-Based Models Jos Carlos Rosales Nez, Guillaume Wisniewski, Djamé Seddah. In proceedings of the 7th W-NUT workshop colocated with EMNLP 2021 . preprint
  • Can Character-based Language Models Improve Downstream Task Performance in Low-Resource and Noisy Language Scenarios?, Arij Riabi, Benot Sagot, Djamé Seddah. In proceedings of the 7th W-NUT workshop colocated with EMNLP 2021 (to appear). preprint
  • Synthetic Data Augmentation for Zero-Shot Cross-Lingual Question Answering Arij Riabi, Thomas Scialom, Rachel Keraron, Benot Sagot, Djamé Seddah, Jacopo Staiano. 2021. In Proceedings of EMNLP 2021 . preprint
  • First Align, then Predict: Understanding the Cross-Lingual Ability of Multilingual BERT Benjamin Muller, Yanai Elazar, Benot Sagot and Djamé Seddah. In Proceedings of EACL 2021 (Virtual). pdf
  • When Being Unseen from mBERT is just the Beginning: Handling New Languages With Multilingual Language Models Benjamin Muller, Antonis Anastasopoulos, Benot Sagot and Djamé Seddah. In proceedings of NAACL 2021 (Virtual) pdf

2020

  • Treebanking User-Generated Content: a UD Based Overview of Guidelines, Corpora and Unified Recommendations Manuela Sanguinetti, Lauren Cassidy, Cristina Bosco, zlem etinoğlu, Alessandra Teresa Cignarella, Teresa Lynn, Ines Rehbein, Josef Ruppenhofer, Djamé Seddah, Amir Zeldes. In The 58th Annual Meeting of the Association for Computational Linguistics (ACL 2020), Seattle, Washington, United States website preprint

2019

2018

2017

2009

2008

2007

2006

  • Modlisation et analyse des coordinations elliptiques via l’exploitation dynamique des forts de drivation, (Modeling and analysis of elleptic coordination through the dynamic exploitation of derivation forests), Traitement automatique des Langues Naturelles 2006 (TALN06), Louvain, Belgique, D. Seddah & B. Sagot
  • Modeling and Analysis of Elliptic Coordination by Dynamic Exploitation of Derivation Forests in LTAG Parsing. Seddah Djam et Sagot Benot (2006). In Actes de TAG+8, Sydney, Australia.

2005

  • How to Build Argumental graphs Using TAG Shared Forest : a view from control verbs , Logical Aspect of Computional Linguistic (LACL) 2005, Bordeaux, Avril 2005, D.Seddah & B.Gaiffe
  • Des arbres de drivation aux forts de dpendance : un chemin via les forts partages ,(From Derivation Trees to Dependency Forests : A Path Trough Shared Forests), Traitement automatique des langues Naturelles (TALN) 2005, Dourdan, Juin 2005, D.Seddah & B.Gaiffe

2004

  • Synchronisation des connaissances syntaxiques et smantiques pour l’analyse d’noncs en langage naturel partir du formalisme des Grammaires d’Arbres Adjoints” ‘‘Synchronization of syntactical and semantic knowledge for natural language analysis in the Lexicalized Tree Adjoining Grammars framework

PhD in Computer Sciences at the University Henry Poincar (UHP, Nancy, France) Defended the 5th of November 2004’’. Supervised by Jean Marie Pierrel (Pr. University Henry Poincar), Bertrand Gaiffe (Researcher, CNRS)

  • ‘-Referees :
  • Phillipe Blache, Research Director, CNRS & University of Province
  • Christophe Fouquer, Pr. University PARIS Nord (13) & CNRS-LIPN
  • Jean Yves Marion, Pr. Institut National Polytechnique de Lorraine & INRIA-Lorraine
  • Eric Villemonte de la Clergerie, Researcher, INRIA-Rocquencourt
  • Laurence Danlos, Pr. University Paris Denis Diderot (7) & CNRS

2002

  • Conceptualization of a lexical information system , Terminology and Knowledge Enginering (TKE) 2002, Nancy, Aot 2002, E. Jacquey & D.Seddah,
  • Conceptualisation d’un systme d’informations lexicales : une interface paramtrable pour le T.A.L, RECITAL2002, Nancy, Juin 2002, D.Seddah, E. Jacquey

2000

  • Practical aspect in compiling tabular TAG Parsers ,TAG+5, Paris , Mai 2000 M.A Pardo, D. Seddah & E. Villemonte de la Clergerie
  • Performance des correcteurs syntaxiques , ‘‘Linux Pratique n9, Mai 2000

’’

  • tude du moteur de reconnaissance optique de caractres OCR-SHOP , Linux Pratique n11, Juillet 2000

Seminars and Invited Talks

  • Coordination and Control processing using Multi-Component TAG grammar: is this realistic?, November 16th, 2012, Dusseldorf, Germany
  • Vers un treebank des mdias sociaux: l’analyse syntaxique face au web 2.0. LingLunch Paris 7 seminar series, June 2012, Paris
  • Statistical Parsing of French,Seminar INFOGLU, Marne la Valle (Paris 13), France, June 2011
  • Statistical Parsing of MRLs : Lemmatization, Morphological Clustering and Lexicalized Parsing of French, Dublin, Ireland, November 3rd, 2010
  • Cross Parser Evaluation and Tagset Variation : a French Treebanks Study, Dublin, Ireland, December 09, 2009, Feast, Saarland University
  • An Overview on LFG Parsing with XLE , Tutorial, NCLT, Dublin City University, Dublin,27/03/06
  • Tree Adjoining Grammar and Syntax Semantic Interface , NCLT Seminar Series, Dublin City University, Dublin, 18/01/06
  • Fort de drivation et dpendances argumentales dans les grammaires d’arbre adjoint lexicalises : Une approche illustrative sur les verbes contrle et les coordinations elliptiques , Sminaire Traitement automatique des langues, Loria Nancy, le 24 Novembre 2005 et Sminaire Lattice-Talana, Universit Paris 7, Paris, le 21 Novembre 2005
  • Conceptualisation d’un systme d’informations lexicales ,(avec E.Jacquey), Journe de l’Inist : Ingnierie des langues et traitement de l’information , 30 Janvier 2003, Nancy.
